paper_title: Quantum AI for Cancer Diagnostic Biomarker Discovery

P002_S0006.reason: structural precheck: mixed-direction inequality chain in perturbed math

P000_S0032.reason: The surrounding_context explicitly states 'Sample$_3$, representing the combined gene set, achieved the highest overall predictive performance', so changing $_3$ to $_2$ contradicts the stated sample label.

P001_S0035.reason: The surrounding_context shows 'Omic1.1${Subtype-I}$ and Omic1.2${Subtype-II}$', so the perturbed span changes Omic1.1 to be labeled Subtype-II instead of Subtype-I, contradicting the established pairing.

P002_S0037.reason: The surrounding_context and related_passages consistently use < α (e.g., AdjP < α) as the significance threshold; changing ≤ 0.05 to ≥ 0.05 reverses the filtering direction, retaining non-significant genes instead of significant ones.

P003_S0039.reason: The surrounding_context explicitly uses LUAD$_{TN}$ (Tumor vs Normal) twice in the same passage; changing TN to TT contradicts the established notation for the comparison.

P000_S0000.reason: The surrounding_context shows the original requires AdjP_LUSC_TN ≥ α (gene NOT significant in LUSC) to define LUAD-specificity; changing to < α makes the condition require LUSC significance, contradicting the LUAD-specific definition.

P001_S0002.reason: The surrounding_context shows the original formula uses set difference (U_1 \ U_2) to exclude genes significant in LUSC; replacing with union (U_1 ∪ U_2) includes LUSC-significant genes, contradicting the LUAD-specific hypermethylation objective.

P002_S0046.reason: The surrounding_context establishes that significance means p_adj < 0.05; changing to > 0.05 reverses the condition, flagging non-significant genes as significant, contradicting standard statistical practice and the downstream use of S_{g,k}.

P003_S0009.reason: The surrounding_context shows the original uses max(S_{g,F1}, S_{g,F3}), meaning a gene is LUAD-significant if significant in either F1 or F3; changing to min requires significance in both, contradicting the OR-logic intended.

P000_S0011.reason: The original third case requires S_LUAD=0 and/or S_LUSC=0 (at least one is non-significant), but the perturbed version requires S_LUAD=1 and/or S_LUSC=1, making it overlap with the first two cases and contradicting the intended exhaustive classification.
P000_S0011.original:
\[
\begin{cases}
\text{LUAD-specific}, & \text{if } S_{\mathrm{LUAD}} = 1 \text{ and } S_{\mathrm{LUSC}} = 0, \\
\text{Opposite both significant}, & \text{if } S_{\mathrm{LUAD}} = 1 \text{ and } S_{\mathrm{LUSC}} = 1, \\
\text{Opposite weak}, & \text{if } S_{\mathrm{LUAD}} = 0 \text{ and/or } S_{\mathrm{LUSC}} = 0.
\end{cases}
\]
P000_S0011.perturbed:
\[
\begin{cases}
\text{LUAD-specific}, & \text{if } S_{\mathrm{LUAD}} = 1 \text{ and } S_{\mathrm{LUSC}} = 0, \\
\text{Opposite both significant}, & \text{if } S_{\mathrm{LUAD}} = 1 \text{ and } S_{\mathrm{LUSC}} = 1, \\
\text{Opposite weak}, & \text{if } S_{\mathrm{LUAD}} = 1 \text{ and/or } S_{\mathrm{LUSC}} = 1.
\end{cases}
\]

P001_S0014.reason: The surrounding_context states ε is added to avoid division by zero; subtracting ε instead could make the denominator zero or negative when -log10(p_adj,F2,g) equals ε, contradicting the stated purpose of ε.

P003_S0049.reason: The surrounding_context shows the original uses median(X_j) where j indexes features/columns; changing to X_i (row/sample index) means imputing with the median across features for a sample rather than the median across samples for a feature, contradicting standard column-wise imputation.

P002_S0017.reason: The surrounding_context explicitly states 'Data were split into 80% training and 20% testing', so test_size=0.8 contradicts the stated 20% test split.

P003_S0019.reason: The surrounding_context shows both X_DNA and X_RNA have 128 features each, and the test set concatenation yields ×256; the perturbed train set dimension of 128 contradicts the concatenation of two 128-feature matrices which must yield 256.

P000_S0043.reason: The standard F1-score formula requires the factor 2 to make it the harmonic mean of precision and recall; changing 2 to 1 produces a different (incorrect) formula contradicting the stated definition of F1 as the harmonic mean.
